findings.title: CSP domain wildcard uses placeholder brand name, not actual production domain
findings.description: The Content-Security-Policy includes https://*.PDH.com in script-src, connect-src, and frame-src. 'PDH.com' is a placeholder — the app's actual production domain is presumably a .vercel.app URL or a real registered domain. If PDH.com is unregistered, this directive is functionally dead (harmless), but it signals the CSP was not updated to a real domain. Worse, if a third party ever registers PDH.com, wildcard trust would be granted to any subdomain they control. The previous value was https://*.caca-traca.com, which was equally a placeholder — this PR renamed the placeholder but did not fix the underlying problem.
findings.fix: Replace https://*.PDH.com with the actual production domain once it is registered (e.g. https://*.getpdh.com or whatever the real domain is). Until then, remove the placeholder entry entirely — the vercel.app wildcard already covers Vercel preview deployments.

findings.title: PWA manifest declares screenshot files that do not exist on disk
findings.description: The web app manifest declares two install-prompt screenshots: /tracking-mobile-view.png (line 80) and /patterns-mobile.png (line 86). Neither file exists in public/ — both were deleted as part of this PR's landing page and marketing asset strip. An invalid manifest screenshot causes install-prompt failures and will fail PWA audits (Lighthouse, browser DevTools manifest inspector). This is an actively broken state, not a hypothetical.
findings.fix: Either remove the screenshots array from the manifest entirely (screenshots are optional per the spec and their absence will not break installation), or replace both entries with existing public/ images such as /tracking-personalisation-img.png. If the install-prompt experience matters, source and add appropriate replacement screenshots.

findings.title: PWA manifest references icon sizes that do not exist on disk
findings.description: The manifest icons array includes icons/icon-384x384.png and icons/icon-512x512.png. Neither exists in public/icons/. The full set of icons on disk is: 72x72, 96x96, 128x128, 144x144, 152x152, 192x192. Chrome's PWA installability check requires at least one icon at 512x512 or larger — without it, the browser will refuse to show the install prompt entirely. This is a regression from the pre-PR state (these icons were present before the asset strip).
findings.fix: Regenerate and add icons/icon-384x384.png and icons/icon-512x512.png to public/icons/. Alternatively, update the manifest to only list sizes that actually exist on disk and source or generate a 512x512 icon to satisfy the installability requirement.

findings.title: CSP uses 'unsafe-inline' for script-src without acknowledgement
findings.description: 'unsafe-inline' in script-src negates the XSS protection that CSP would otherwise provide. This is a known limitation of Vite SPAs (Vite injects an inline module preload script), but the current config silently carries this weakness from the original setup without documentation. This PR was a good opportunity to either fix it or explicitly acknowledge it as accepted risk.
findings.fix: If XSS protection is a priority, investigate using a CSP nonce via a Vercel Edge Function middleware or explore Vite's experimental nonce support. At minimum, add an inline comment in vercel.json explaining that 'unsafe-inline' is required by Vite's module preload injection and is an accepted, documented risk.

findings.title: Permissions-Policy blocks microphone but voice logging is a planned feature
findings.description: The Permissions-Policy header sets microphone=(). This prevents the browser from granting microphone access at all — including to the app itself. Project memory (project_logging_ux_pivot.md) documents that voice/conversational logging is the next major UX priority. Deploying this policy now will cause a silent, confusing breakage when voice logging is implemented. The developer building the voice feature may not immediately connect the browser mic-denial to a server-side response header.
findings.fix: Update to microphone=(self) to allow microphone access from the app's own origin while blocking it from third-party frames. Add a comment: '# microphone=(self) required for voice logging feature'. If voice logging is genuinely months away, at minimum add a TODO comment to this line.

findings.title: Workbox globPatterns excludes PNG — PWA precache misses all image assets
findings.description: The workbox precache uses globPatterns: ['**/*.{js,css,html,ico,svg}']. This excludes .png, .webp, and .jpg. The public/ directory contains PNG images the app actively uses (app-data-img.png, health-surgery-img.png, tracking-personalisation-img.png). These will always require a network request. The runtimeCaching rule for images with StaleWhileRevalidate partially mitigates this, but only after the first load — they are never precached for offline-first reliability. This is pre-existing, not introduced by this PR, but the PR removed marketing screenshots while leaving this gap.
findings.fix: Update to globPatterns: ['**/*.{js,css,html,ico,svg,png,webp,jpg}']. If images are intentionally excluded from precache to keep the SW install payload small, document this as a deliberate trade-off.

findings.title: dotenv listed as production dependency but is only used in e2e test setup
findings.description: dotenv is in the dependencies block (production). The only file in the entire codebase that imports it is e2e/auth.setup.ts, a Playwright test setup file that runs only during CI testing. Vite handles env variable loading natively at build time via import.meta.env — dotenv is not needed in the production bundle and should never be. This adds an unnecessary production dependency.
findings.fix: Move dotenv to devDependencies. Run bun install to update the lockfile.

findings.title: next-themes in production dependencies but not imported anywhere in src/
findings.description: next-themes is listed in dependencies but a grep of the entire src/ tree finds zero imports of 'next-themes'. The codebase uses a custom src/components/theme-provider.tsx that reimplements theme switching with vanilla React (no next-themes dependency). This is a dead dependency that contributes to bundle size and increases the npm audit surface area for no benefit.
findings.fix: Remove next-themes from package.json dependencies and run bun install to clean up.

findings.title: gitignore retains transit map directory exclusions for a deleted feature
findings.description: Lines 59-67 contain entries for 'transit map/IMG_4190.HEIC', 'transit map/transit map image.png', and similar transit map design asset files. The transit map feature has been fully deleted in this PR. The directory does not exist. These entries are dead and mislead future readers into thinking the transit map directory might still appear.
findings.fix: Remove lines 59-67 from .gitignore. The transit map directory no longer exists and will not reappear.

findings.title: E2E tests do not cover the unauthenticated redirect path after landing page deletion
findings.description: Previously the landing page served as the unauthenticated root — visiting / without auth showed the marketing page with a Sign In button. With LandingPage.tsx deleted, the unauthenticated path has changed. The current e2e tests only validate the authenticated state. There is no test confirming what an unauthenticated user sees at / after this PR — which is the highest-risk behavioral change in the entire strip. An unauthenticated user hitting a blank page or an unhandled error would be a regression.
findings.fix: Add a test for the unauthenticated flow. Using a storageState-free browser context, navigate to / and assert the user is redirected to Clerk's sign-in page (or the correct auth gate), and that no unhandled error is displayed. This can be done in a new describe block within app-loads.spec.ts or a new spec file.

findings.title: Page <title> is bare 'PDH' — not descriptive for tabs or bookmarks
findings.description: The <title> is now 'PDH' after the rename. While brand-consistent, it provides no context in browser tabs, bookmark managers, or screen reader page announcements. The previous 'Caca Traca' was equally non-descriptive, so this is not a regression — but the rename was an opportunity to improve discoverability and accessibility.
findings.fix: Consider 'PDH — Digestion Tracker' or 'PDH | Food & Digestion Log'. This also improves accessibility: screen readers announce the title on navigation, and a descriptive title helps users with multiple tabs open.

findings.title: Package name 'PDH' is uppercase — non-standard for npm package names
findings.description: The npm spec requires package names to be lowercase. While this is a private package (private: true) that will never be published to the registry, uppercase names can cause issues with bun's lockfile resolution, monorepo tooling, and some CI environments that normalise package names. The previous name 'caca-traca' was valid lowercase-kebab-case.
findings.fix: Change to "name": "pdh" or "name": "pdh-app". This is a cosmetic fix for a private package but removes a potential tooling friction point.

findings.title: ThemeProvider storageKey retains stale internal name 'kaka-tracker-theme'
findings.description: This finding is in src/main.tsx (outside the 7 reviewed files), but was directly caused by the rename done in this PR. The ThemeProvider's storageKey prop is 'kaka-tracker-theme' — the original internal project name. This key is written to localStorage to persist the user's theme preference. It was not updated alongside the package.json, index.html, and tsconfig renames, making it an incomplete rename.
findings.fix: Update the storageKey in src/main.tsx to 'pdh-theme'. Note: existing users will lose their stored theme preference on next load and revert to the dark default. This is acceptable for a development-stage app.

findings.title: Individual .playwright-cli timestamped filenames hardcoded in gitignore
findings.description: Lines 51-57 list individual timestamped .playwright-cli/*.yml files by name. Line 58 adds a catch-all .playwright-cli/ directory entry that already covers them. The individual filename entries are redundant and create maintenance noise each time a playwright-cli session generates a new file (requiring a manual gitignore update).
findings.fix: Remove lines 51-57. The .playwright-cli/ entry on line 58 is sufficient to ignore all files in that directory.
